children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: Each Federal financial institutions regulatory agency and the Resolution Trust Corporation shall prescribe, in accordance with sections 3342 and 3343 of this title, which categories of federally related transactions should be appraised by a State certified appraiser and which by a State licensed appraiser under this chapter.

children.heading: Threshold level
children.label: b Threshold level
children.text: Each Federal financial institutions regulatory agency and the Resolution Trust Corporation may establish a threshold level at or below which a certified or licensed appraiser is not required to perform appraisals in connection with federally related transactions, if such agency determines in writing that such threshold level does not represent a threat to the safety and soundness of financial institutions, and receives concurrence from the Bureau of Consumer Financial Protection that such threshold level provides reasonable protection for consumers who purchase 1–4 unit single-family residences.

children.heading: GAO study of appraisals in connection with real estate related financial transactions below threshold level
children.label: c GAO study of appraisals in connection with real estate related financial transactions below threshold level
A
children.text: the cost to any financial institution involved in any such transaction;

children.label: B
children.text: the possibility of losses to the Deposit Insurance Fund or the National Credit Union Share Insurance Fund;

children.label: C
children.text: the cost to any customer involved in any such transaction; and

children.heading: Reports to Congress and the appropriate Federal financial institutions regulatory agencies
children.label: 2 Reports to Congress and the appropriate Federal financial institutions regulatory agencies
children.text: Upon completing each of the studies referred to in paragraph (1), the Comptroller General shall submit a report on the Comptroller General’s findings and conclusions with respect to such study to the Federal financial institutions regulatory agencies, the Committee on Banking, Finance and Urban Affairs of the House of Representatives, and the Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs of the Senate, together with such recommendations for legislative or administrative action as the Comptroller General determines to be appropriate.
